evidence.scientific_clarity: All four panels (method diagram, ablation bars, training curves, sensitivity heatmap) are clearly drawn and directly support the brief's claims; panel labels A-D in bold sans-serif as specified.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set anywhere in the script; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans fonts — not editable/selectable Type 1 or TrueType; DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default and was not changed.
evidence.palette: Blue/gray for Ours/Baseline is direction-appropriate; ablation uses green for 'full' and light-blue for ablated conditions which is sensible; green is not misused for directional up/down; viridis heatmap is colorblind-safe; overall reasonably CB-safe but green is used for both 'best' accent and could conflict with red-green deficiency context.
evidence.layout_density: GridSpec(2,1) with nested 1×3 top row matches the brief; bottom heatmap spans full width; no obvious gaping whitespace; bar values annotated; panels fill their allocated space well.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure content closely; legend in panel C placed upper-right (reasonable); panel A conceptual diagram is schematic but functional; the residual skip arrow is somewhat cramped and the arrow from Skill Router to Output has a coordinate bug (goes slightly backward); overall passable but minor layout quirks remain.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF uses Type 3 (bitmapped) DejaVuSans fonts per pdffonts — text is not selectable/editable; script never sets matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42 or plt.rcParams equivalent, so vector fidelity is compromised.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (50 KB), figure.png (272 KB), figure.svg (109 KB), caption.tex (824 B), and gen_figure.py (7.3 KB) all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains each panel with specific detail; includes a numerical claim ('64.7%' full-model accuracy) and describes the direction of findings (faster convergence, lower final loss); all four panels are addressed.

overall_strengths: Complete four-panel layout matches brief exactly (GridSpec 2×3 with dominant panel A, full-width panel D) and every panel carries a clear scientific message.
Caption is thorough and publication-quality, providing a numerical anchor (64.7%) and directional language for each panel.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is never set, so the PDF embeds Type 3 bitmapped DejaVu Sans fonts instead of editable/selectable TrueType — a critical vector-fidelity failure for journal submission.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout with no explicit font choice; combined with the missing fonttype setting this violates both typography requirements.
